NCTC Director Joe Kent resigns saying 'Iran posed no imminent threat' — first senior US official to publicly dissent on war justification. Bushehr NPP hit. IAEA blocked from Isfahan facility. Kent resignation may signal internal US policy fractures but war continues with no diplomatic off-ramp. Nuclear threshold risk remains elevated under regime pressure.

changed_signals.de_escalation_watch: SIGNAL: Iran easing Hormuz restrictions per maritime data — countries cutting individual deals instead of joining US coalition. This is a partial de-escalation on the economic front, though military strikes continue. Europe refuses Hormuz naval deployment. Putin-Trump call today is the key diplomatic variable for Ukraine. Zero ceasefire signals in ME active theaters.

latest_news.headline: NCTC Director Joe Kent resigns saying 'Iran posed no imminent threat'
latest_news.text: Joe Kent resigns as National Counterterrorism Center director, saying Iran posed no imminent threat to the US and blaming Israel lobby for pushing America into war. First senior Trump official to publicly dissent on war justification.

latest_news.headline: Iran fires revenge missile barrage at Israel — cluster munitions over central Israel
latest_news.text: IRGC launches multi-warhead missiles + cluster munitions at central Israel in revenge for Larijani assassination. 2 killed in Ramat Gan (couple in 70s unable to reach safe room). Shrapnel at Tel Aviv train station.

latest_news.headline: Israel strikes kill 11 in western Iran (Dorud, Chegeni) + judiciary facility in Larestan
latest_news.text: Israeli strikes hit Dorud city (7 killed, 56 injured) and Chegeni region (4 killed) in western Iran. Judiciary facility in Larestan also struck. South Pars gas field targeted.

latest_news.headline: Hormuz traffic nearly doubled — countries cutting individual deals with Iran
latest_news.text: Maritime data shows nearly double the ships transiting Hormuz in recent days. Iran allowing passage for non-US/Israeli vessels. Countries cutting individual deals. Bulk carriers rerouting through Iranian territorial waters. US Hormuz coalition gets zero commitments.

latest_news.headline: Pakistan-Afghanistan agree to 5-day Eid ceasefire starting tonight
latest_news.text: Both sides announce 5-day pause (Mar 18-23) for Eid al-Fitr, mediated by Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Turkey. Pakistan warns operations resume 'with renewed intensity' if attacked. First bilateral de-escalation since conflict began.

outlook: CRITICAL (Day 24, 18:04 UTC) — (1) NCTC Director Kent resigns: 'Iran posed no imminent threat' — internal US dissent on war justification; (2) Iran revenge barrage continues with cluster munitions on Israel; (3) Hormuz traffic doubling — countries bypassing US coalition with individual Iran deals; (4) Pakistan-Afghanistan Eid ceasefire starts tonight — first pause in weeks; (5) Putin-Trump call today on Ukraine — diplomatic variable; (6) Oil ~$108-110/barrel. Hormuz de-escalation on economic front but military escalation continues.
